message:
feat(http): add configurable max body size and detailed BodyTooLarge errors

The HTTP/1.1 client previously hard-coded a 16 MiB maximum response body
size via DEFAULT_MAX_BODY_SIZE, with no way for callers to override it.
This made it impossible to download large files (e.g., binary releases,
database dumps, media assets) without hitting an opaque "body exceeds
size limit" error that gave no indication of the actual vs. allowed size.

This changeset addresses both problems:

1. **Configurable max body size** — A new `max_body_size` field is added
   to `HttpClientConfig` (defaulting to `None`, which preserves the
   existing 16 MiB codec default). `HttpClientBuilder::max_body_size()`
   lets callers raise (or lower) the limit per-client. Internally, a new
   `Http1Client::request_streaming_with_max_body_size()` method threads
   the limit through to both `response_body_kind()` (which checks
   Content-Length up front) and `ClientIncomingBody::with_max_body_size()`
   (which enforces the limit during streaming reads). The original
   `request_streaming()` and `ClientIncomingBody::new()` methods are
   preserved as thin wrappers that pass the default, so all existing
   call sites remain unchanged.

2. **BodyTooLargeDetailed error variant** — A new `HttpError` variant
   `BodyTooLargeDetailed { actual: u64, limit: u64 }` is added alongside
   the existing `BodyTooLarge`. The `response_body_kind()` function now
   returns this detailed variant when Content-Length exceeds the limit,
   so error messages include both the actual body size and the configured
   cap in both raw bytes and human-readable MB (e.g., "body size
   (52428800 bytes, 50.0 MB) exceeds limit (16777216 bytes, 16.0 MB)").
   This makes debugging size-limit rejections straightforward instead of
   requiring users to cross-reference logs with configuration.

3. **Proxy path coverage** — Both the direct-connect and proxy-connect
   code paths in `HttpClient` (`send_request_streaming` and
   `send_request_streaming_via_proxy`) now check `config.max_body_size`
   and dispatch to the appropriate method, ensuring the limit applies
   uniformly regardless of connection mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
